scenarios.opening_query: The camper we bought for retirement was 'winterized and ready' — the first freeze split every pipe.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The sellers were a retiring couple at a campground, cash and a handshake — the dealer-protection statutes don't reach them.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: A retiring couple at a campground — cash and a handshake.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: An RV dealership sold it 'certified winterized' with a checklist they signed — dealer accountability applies.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: An RV dealer sold it 'certified winterized' with a signed checklist.

scenarios.opening_query: My son's first car got 'flagged' at emissions and the state won't renew the tags no matter what we fix.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > DMV (Non-Criminal) || Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes
scenarios.hidden_fact: The flag is a tampered emissions system — the dealer sold it with the defeat device installed, which is their violation to answer for, not our smog problem.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The flag is a tampered emissions system — the dealer sold it with a defeat device installed. That's their violation.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the seller installed illegal equipment
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The car's clean now — the state's system keeps rejecting our valid retest, a records appeal.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It passes now — the state system keeps rejecting the valid retest.

scenarios.opening_query: We financed a car seven months ago and the lender says the dealer never sent them the title.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes || Bankruptcy > Creditors
scenarios.hidden_fact: The dealership just filed Chapter 7 with dozens of unperfected titles floating — we're in the creditor pool fighting for our own car's title through the trustee.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The dealer filed Chapter 7 with dozens of titles floating — we're creditors fighting for our own car's title through the trustee.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the dealer's bankruptcy holds the title
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The dealer's open and solvent — they just sat on the paperwork, and the dealer-practices complaint shakes it loose.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They're open and solvent — just sitting on paperwork.

scenarios.opening_query: A private seller's 'perfect' truck died at 80 mph on the highway with my kids in the back.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Problems Between Consumers || General Litigation > Products Liability
scenarios.hidden_fact: The failure was the manufacturer's recalled brake-booster — never repaired despite two recall notices to previous owners; the maker and the unrepaired-recall chain are the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was the recalled brake-booster — never fixed despite two recall notices to prior owners. The manufacturer chain is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an open recall failure caused the danger
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No recall involved — the seller had sanded the check-engine bulb and knew the engine was going; private-sale fraud.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No recall — he'd disabled the check-engine light knowing the engine was going.

scenarios.opening_query: The dealership's 'guaranteed trade-in value' voucher turned out to be worth nothing when we returned.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a mailer campaign — the same 'guarantee' went to thousands of households with the weasel words microprinted; the advertising practice, not our single deal, is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The same 'guarantee' mailer went to thousands with microprinted weasel words — the advertising practice is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a mass deceptive-advertising practice is at issue
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Ours was a personally negotiated written guarantee on our specific VIN — a direct dealer-contract breach.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Ours was negotiated in writing for our specific VIN — a direct breach.

scenarios.opening_query: I sold my old work van online and now the buyer's lawyer says I'm liable for his crash.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Problems Between Consumers || General Litigation > Personal Injury
scenarios.hidden_fact: The crash claim is serious — he alleges the brakes I 'serviced' failed and he's injured; defending the injury suit is the case, and my old receipts are the evidence.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He alleges the brakes I 'serviced' failed and injured him — defending the injury suit is the case, and my receipts are the evidence.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an injury suit rather than a sale dispute is live
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nobody's hurt — the 'crash' bent a rim, and his letter is a $1,800 demand dressed in litigation costume.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No injury — a bent rim and an $1,800 demand in litigation costume.
scenarios.vetting_note: Injury-vs-demand posture on the seller side.
